user_query:
I'm triaging parent emails for intakes at our children's therapy center. I exported a small batch of messages to plain text files under input/emails. Please build a command-line script that I can run locally to parse these emails, filter out non-actionable messages, rank the actionable ones, and draft initial replies.

Requirements
1) Implementation and how to run
- Implement a CLI script (e.g., scripts/triage.py) and run it to produce the outputs below. Use a single command of the form:
  python3 scripts/triage.py --in-dir input/emails --out-dir output
- The script must read every file in input/emails and produce deterministic outputs in the output directory.

2) Parsing and extraction
- Each email is a plain text file with email-style headers (From, To, Subject, Date, Message-ID, and sometimes Auto-Submitted) followed by a body.
- Some emails include an "---- Intake Details ----" block with key-value lines. Extract these fields when present:
  - Child Name
  - Age (as an integer)
  - Preferred Times (free text)
  - Completed Intake (Yes/No)
- Also extract from headers:
  - message_id (Message-ID header value, without surrounding < > if present)
  - from_email (email address from the From header)
  - subject
  - date_iso (normalize the Date header into ISO 8601 with time zone)
- If an intake field is missing, leave it empty in the output.

3) Filtering (include/exclude)
- Exclude automatic replies and bounces if ANY of the following is true (case-insensitive):
  - Subject contains: "out of office", "automatic reply", "delivery status notification", or "mail delivery failed"
  - Header Auto-Submitted is present with any value
- Include only messages that (after exclusion above) meet at least one of:
  - Subject contains any of: "intake", "waitlist", or "evaluation" (case-insensitive)
  - Body contains the delimiter line "---- Intake Details ----"

4) Scoring, sorting, and ranking
- For each included message, compute a score:
  - +3 if Completed Intake is "Yes"
  - +2 if Age is an integer <= 7
  - +1 if subject contains "evaluation" (case-insensitive)
- Sort included messages by: score DESC, then date_iso ASC (older first for ties).
- Assign a 1-based rank after sorting.

5) Outputs
- output/triage_queue.csv (UTF-8): one row per included message with columns in this exact order:
  message_id, from_email, subject, date_iso, child_name, child_age, preferred_times, completed_intake, score, rank, reason_tags
  - reason_tags is a semicolon-separated list of the triggers that contributed to the score, using these tag names when applicable: has_completed_intake, young_child, evaluation_keyword
- output/drafts/: create one .txt file per top-3 ranked messages, named <message_id>.txt. Each draft should be a brief, plain-text reply that:
  - Greets the sender by child_name if available, otherwise uses a generic greeting.
  - Acknowledges receipt on the message's date (use date_iso).
  - If completed_intake == Yes: thank them for completing the intake and note that scheduling review is next.
  - If completed_intake == No: ask them to complete our intake packet (you can say "I will send the packet in a follow-up").
  - Does not include any external links or attachments.

Please run the script so that output/triage_queue.csv and top-3 draft files under output/drafts are created from the provided inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
